title: The BEST AI for 4K images. Free & fast
summary: PiD Pixel Diffusion by Nvidia is a free and open source AI image model designed for high resolution 4K image generation. It uses text encoders like Gemma 2b and diffusion models optimized for fast inference. The model can be used within ComfyUI workflows for image generation and upscaling tasks.
writeup: Nvidia's Pixel Diffusion (PID) is a new open-source image upscaler that operates in pixel space rather than latent space: instead of decoding a compressed representation, it takes a latent from a base model such as Flux or Stable Diffusion as guidance and directly denoises the image at high resolution, producing 4K output in under a second. The video demonstrates PID against the leading alternative SeedVR2, showing sharper edges, more faithful textures, and up to 5.9x faster throughput. The second half is a hands-on ComfyUI installation walkthrough covering three workflows: text-to-image at 1K, image-to-2K/4K upscaling, and a pipeline that chains Flux or Z Image generation directly into PID for a single-pass high-resolution result.
key_points: PID denoises directly in high-resolution pixel space rather than using a traditional latent-space decoder, which reduces the artifacts common in conventional upscaling and produces sharper 4K results with fewer hallucinated details.

Against SeedVR2, the previous leading open-source upscaler, PID is up to 5.9x faster and wins the majority of side-by-side quality comparisons; the full BF16 model weighs only 2.7 GB, and the compressed MXFP8 variant (for Blackwell-architecture GPUs) is 1.5 GB.

Three ComfyUI workflows ship with the release: workflow O1 generates 1K images from text, O2 upscales an existing image to 2K or 4K, and O3 chains a base model (Flux or Z Image) with PID so the base model's latent feeds directly into PID for a higher-resolution final output.

Installation requires three downloads: the Gemma 2 2B text encoder (5.2 GB FP16 or 2.6 GB FP8, placed in models/text_encoders), the PID diffusion model matched to the latent type of the intended base model (e.g., Flux 1 for Z Image, placed in models/diffusion_models), and a VAE such as AE.safetensors (335 MB, placed in models/VAE).

ComfyUI must be updated to the latest version before loading the workflow; outdated installs show red-outlined nodes and fail to run, and the update is handled by running update_comfyui.bat from the portable ComfyUI folder.
